purpose: AirAccount #70 / #68 payload-commitment golden vector. Proves the WebAuthn challenge the user's passkey signs commits to EXACTLY the userOpHash that the DVT nodes co-sign (BLS) and the KMS signs (secp256k1). This is the C1 hard constraint made executable: the user-authorized op, the DVT-co-signed op, and the KMS-signed op are the same op. NOTE: this is NOT a BLS vector and AirAccount is NOT a 4th BLS signer — the BLS hashToG2/pkAgg vectors are owned by node(#42)/SDK(#63)/contract(#110).
source_of_truth: kms/ta/src/main.rs verify_challenge_binding (#68): hasher.update(nonce); hasher.update(payload); committed = finalize(). payload = userOpHash (32 bytes), nonce = TA-issued one-time challenge (32 bytes).
nonce_note: Fixed 32-byte TEST nonce for this known-answer vector ONLY. Real challenges use a random, TA-issued, one-time nonce from GetChallenge — never this constant.
alignment: u0/u1 are the SAME canonical userOpHash values as airaccount-contract test/HashToG2Golden.t.sol (vec1/vec2). So the #68 user-authorization binding (this file) and the on-chain BLS hashToG2 vectors refer to byte-identical operations — closing the cross-repo loop on a shared op identity.
